metadata.title: Claude, Oversimplified — Graded
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent (spark, /skill token, active callout arrow); callout ink #161511 on cream
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Swahili. Wagwan check: charsum('claude-oversimplified') % 10 == 3, not 0.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 opens directly on the Claude UI (ClaudeComposerAsk) — never a brand open card. Spine: B00 ASK · B01–B06 one poster claim per beat (claude-window + claude-callout, callout N reveals at beat start) · B07 VERDICT (v2 'still oversimplified' poster) · B08 outro. Source poster: Ruben Hassid, 'How to AI'. OUTRO LAW: the outro restates the episode title, poster-style serif, handle beneath — never a generic brand outro. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats never show a lonely asterisk — the spark carries one short serif line summarizing the beat. HANDOFF LAW: second-to-last beat = composer with a paste-ready prompt (greeting 'Your turn.'); typing in cold open and handoff only.
metadata.description_blurb: Six poster claims about Claude — graded. What holds, what oversells, and what the poster skips entirely.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Five of the poster's six claims hold: editing real files, building an app tonight, saving skills, and AskUserQuestion all check out. Claude reads big folders selectively, like a librarian not a scanner, which is a feature to understand. 'Smartest AI on earth' is marketing, and setting effort to High burns usage when Medium suits most work. What the poster skips matters more: Claude keeps working after you close the laptop and returns answers as files.

artifactLines: Five of the poster's six claims hold: editing real files, building an app tonight, saving skills, and AskUserQuestion all check out.
Claude reads big folders selectively, like a librarian not a scanner, which is a feature to understand.
'Smartest AI on earth' is marketing, and setting effort to High burns usage when Medium suits most work.
What the poster skips matters more: Claude keeps working after you close the laptop and returns answers as files.

beats.narration_text: What I keep circling back to is 'the code exists, and you'll meet it the first time it breaks.' That honesty makes me want to build something small tonight anyway. Help me (1) make a four-minute app for one annoying task I have, knowing I'll meet the code when it breaks, (2) turn the repeated part into a saved skill with slash skill-creator, and (3) point Claude at a real folder so the edited spreadsheet comes back saved on my own disk.

props.command: What I keep circling back to is 'the code exists, and you'll meet it the first time it breaks.' That honesty makes me want to build something small tonight anyway. Help me (1) make a four-minute app for one annoying task I have, knowing I'll meet the code when it breaks, (2) turn the repeated part into a saved skill with slash skill-creator, and (3) point Claude at a real folder so the edited spreadsheet comes back saved on my own disk.

beats.narration_text: Claim one: no more copy-paste. Real. Point Claude at a folder and it edits the actual files — the spreadsheet that comes back is your spreadsheet, saved on your disk.
Make an app tonight — four minutes, zero lines of code. Zero lines you write. The code exists, and you'll meet it the first time it breaks. Tonight is the honest part.
Teach it once. A skill is a saved playbook, and slash skill-creator does exactly this. Most underrated arrow on the poster. Steal this one.
End every request with AskUserQuestion, and Claude interviews you — real tool, real behavior. Small print: Cowork already asks before big work. The magic word earns its keep in Chat and Code.
Drop in anything — the messy doc, last month's numbers. Mostly true. On a big folder it reads selectively — a librarian, not a scanner. That's a feature. Just know it.
The smartest AI on earth — that's marketing, not a measurement. And effort on High, that's the whole setup, mis-sets the one knob it mentions. High burns usage. Medium is right for most work.
Verdict: five of six hold. What the poster skips matters more — it keeps working after you close the laptop, it asks before it acts, and answers come back as files. That's the next video.
Don't take my grades for it — your turn. Take this prompt and let Claude grade the poster against your own work.

props.command: which of the poster's six claims actually applies to my work? grade them with me. AskUserQuestion.
